spec.title: CLI parity for spec commands — the full flow without the button

tasks.title: Managed-block engine (docsManagedBlock.js) with tests
tasks.whatChanged: Created src/shared/docsManagedBlock.js — a pure string-surgery engine: findBlock parses the versioned `frame:managed:spec-section` HTML-comment markers, upgradeDoc replaces a stamped-older block in place (every byte outside the span preserved), and a legacy-migration path replaces a heading-bounded section only when its text byte-matches (whitespace-normalized) a shipped Frame generation. test/docsManagedBlock.test.js covers version gating, once-only migration, customized-body no-op, and marker corruption.
tasks.whyChanged: The engine is the safety core of the docs upgrade: it must never touch user prose. Beyond the plan, any marker fragment without a well-formed block now blocks legacy migration entirely — a dangling marker means corrupted state, and migrating around it could double-wrap a section.

tasks.title: Current-generation docs content in frameTemplates.js
tasks.whatChanged: Added SPEC_SECTION_VERSION (1) to src/shared/frameTemplates.js, preserved the old spec sections byte-for-byte as LEGACY_SPEC_DRIVEN_SECTION / LEGACY_SPEC_DRIVEN_CORE_SECTION and exported them as per-doc migration matchers. Replaced SPEC_DRIVEN_SECTION with the full self-serve protocol (deterministic target-spec rule, override-first template resolution, placeholder table, exact-follow rule, autonomous ceiling with the dual Frame-button/implement-launch.js handoff) and SPEC_DRIVEN_CORE_SECTION with the short pointer plus the never-run-from-memory line.
tasks.whyChanged: The legacy texts must stay frozen so the D11 byte-match gate recognizes docs older Frames actually wrote; the new sections are the payload the managed-block engine stamps and upgrades.

tasks.title: Marked emission wired into templates + round-trip tests
tasks.whatChanged: getReferenceTemplate and getAgentsTemplate now emit the spec sections wrapped in managed-block markers stamped SPEC_SECTION_VERSION (new renderSpecSection/renderSpecCoreSection helpers in frameTemplates.js), and enableSpecDriven's append path in frameProject.js appends the wrapped core section. Extended test/docsManagedBlock.test.js with round-trip assertions: freshly emitted docs parse at the current version and are left alone by upgradeDoc; the frozen legacy constants still pass the migration gate.
tasks.whyChanged: The round-trip test exposed a boundary gap: a legacy section followed by a --- separator and non-heading content overran its span, so findLegacySpan now also stops at a thematic break — the docs' own section separator.

tasks.title: Generalized command staging module (commandStaging.js) with tests
tasks.whatChanged: Created src/main/commandStaging.js: pure resolveStagingPlan(projectPath, tool, existsFn) returning {src, dst, executable?} entries for the four spec templates plus plan-report-template.html and build-implement-report.mjs into .frame/runtime/commands/<tool>/ (override-first) and implement-launch.js into .frame/bin/ (packaged only); stageCommandFiles executes the plan for every packaged tool dir via copyIfChanged (moved from specManager) and chmods the helper. test/commandStaging.test.js pins the resolution contract with an injected existsFn.
tasks.whyChanged: Resolution is pure and filesystem-free so the project convention (test pure logic, skip Electron-coupled wrappers) reaches it; specManager/frameProject rewiring is deliberately left to T05 per sequencing.

tasks.title: Rewire staging call sites; retire stageImplementCommandFiles
tasks.whatChanged: specManager.js: deleted stageImplementCommandFiles, copyIfChanged and their constants; the WATCH_SPECS handler and the implement-dispatch branch of buildSpecCommandFile now call commandStaging.stageCommandFiles. frameProject.js: runProjectInit and ensureSpecDrivenArtifacts (both enableSpecDriven branches) call commandStaging.stageCommandFiles, wrapped non-fatal.
tasks.whyChanged: Same paths, superset of files — the v2 implement-only slice is preserved while all four commands become self-servable. Staging was added to ensureSpecDrivenArtifacts rather than enableSpecDriven directly so the already-enabled short-circuit path also restages.
